goal: Let Red Team admins configure and commission voice phishing campaigns against their own employees via a managed VOIP-backed wizard, with AI-generated caller personas and per-call outcome streaming.
user_segments: Dune Operator (execution side, no described UI)
success_metrics: Not defined in PRD — flag for PM
constraints: Vishing-only in v1 — cannot combine with SMS or WhatsApp in same campaign
Admins request campaigns; Dune operators activate delivery — admin cannot trigger calls directly
Voice AEPs non-interchangeable with text AEPs in campaign selection
Remediation suppressed by default; suppression locked at launch
Risk scoring pipeline isolated from vishing outcomes in v1
Caller ID spoofing feasibility dependent on Dune VOIP provider capabilities and jurisdiction law
IDP SCIM required for per-target local timezone call window application
assumptions: Dune's VOIP infrastructure places calls automatically using the Voice AEP persona (AI-driven) — not confirmed, contradicted by 'operators actively placing calls' language in Step 5
Call recording consent and jurisdiction compliance checks are platform-verified states — likely self-certifications without a described document store
Call outcome classification is entered by Dune operators manually after each call — not described explicitly
Debrief is entirely out-of-platform for v1
Voice AEP locked at campaign activation (Calling), not at submission (Pending Activation)
gaps: Execution model (AI-driven vs. human-operated calls) is contradicted within the PRD — highest-leverage unresolved question
Caller ID spoofing capability not confirmed — Caller Identity fields may be script briefing only, not VOIP-level config
Compliance check verification model not described — no document store, upload flow, or verification mechanism for consent items
No ops-to-admin feedback loop for Pending Activation — ops cannot request changes without going outside the platform
Call outcome classification mechanism not described — latency between call completion and platform update unknown
Campaign edit flow during Pending Activation not described
Susceptibility rate denominator inconsistency: vishing uses Reached, text campaigns use Total Delivered — rates non-comparable
Debrief flow absent — no in-platform disclosure or notification tool described
Operator-side UI and permission model not described
No described mechanism for admins without a registered phone number to complete Step 7 test call
edge_cases: VOIP degrades while campaign is in Calling status — auto-pause or manual ops handling?
Test call completes but reviewed checkbox doesn't appear due to VOIP event not received
Zero Reached targets at campaign completion — susceptibility rate 0/0 division edge case
Admin pauses campaign while a call is currently connected — does live call complete or terminate?
Admin re-tags outcome post-campaign — does this retroactively update susceptibility rate in Overview tab?
Two gray badge states (Not Yet Called, No Answer) visually indistinguishable in Call Log table
Voice AEP Channel Type selector — admin builds full text AEP before realizing they need voice; must restart
Large campaign (500+ targets) Call Log table pagination and load performance not described
Export CSV during Calling status — in-progress data inclusion behavior not specified

critical_questions.question: Is VOIP execution AI-driven (automated bot) or human-operated (Dune red teamers using script)? This changes the AEP test flow, campaign scale limits, and outcome streaming model.
Does Dune's VOIP infrastructure support caller ID spoofing to display arbitrary caller name and company on recipient devices? If not, what identity is surfaced?
Where is call recording consent stored and verified? Is the jurisdiction check platform-verified or self-certified?
Is there a mechanism for Dune ops to request changes to a submitted campaign before activating, or can they only activate or contact admin outside the platform?
How are call outcomes classified — VOIP semantic events or manual operator entry in an ops panel? What is the latency between call completion and platform update?
Should campaign edits be allowed while in Pending Activation? Does editing reset the ops review queue?
Should susceptibility rate use Reached or Total Targets as denominator to ensure comparability with text campaign rates?
Is debrief entirely out-of-platform for v1? Should post-campaign reporting include debrief guidance or resources?
When admin pauses a vishing campaign, do in-progress calls (currently connected) terminate immediately or complete naturally?
Is the Voice AEP locked at submission (Pending Activation) or at activation (Calling)?

design_risks: AI-vs-human execution ambiguity creates false expectations about campaign scale and completion speed — admins may submit 200-target campaigns expecting automated overnight completion
Step 4 status card pattern implies platform verification for self-certified compliance items — legal exposure if consent is not actually on file
Pending Activation is a trust vacuum — no status updates, estimated timeline, or change request mechanism for admins waiting on ops
Call outcome classification ambiguity at the boundary between Engaged and Compromised — subjective for voice calls vs. objective for text (link clicked)
Two gray badge states (Not Yet Called and No Answer) break visual scanning in Call Log table at campaign scale
AEP Channel Type selector is a one-way decision with late-visible consequences — admins who choose wrong must rebuild the entire AEP
teaching_notes: Inherit 8-step wizard pattern directly from Red Team Campaign Launcher — design vishing steps as channel-swapped variants, not a new wizard
AEP builder Channel Type selector is net-new — must integrate into existing builder header without disrupting the established two-step flow

Step 4 compliance pre-flight must visually distinguish platform-verified checks (VOIP status) from self-certified items (consent, jurisdiction) — use acknowledgment/checkbox pattern for self-certifications
Re-tag interaction in Call Log tab inherits from text campaign Conversations tab — adapt to vishing outcome labels (Engaged, Compromised, Declined) not text labels
Risk score isolation is confirmed for v1 — no risk delta indicators anywhere in vishing reporting surfaces
Susceptibility rate denominator decision has downstream reporting implications — flag for PM before designing Overview charts
